description: Sparrow with a rather streaky crown (lacked a prominent light-colored median crown stripe). Had a distinct, white eye-ring and a dark post-ocular stripe with a thin white line above it.  The bill was conical.  Upper mandible of bill was dark; lower mandible was pale.  Both the supercilium and sides of the neck were gray with dark streaks (maroon in color).  Auricular patch was grayish-brown in color.  The breast was buffy in color with sparse, diffuse streaking.  Belly was white.  Sides were lightly streaked.  Tail was short and forked.  Bird was facing us most of the time, but I did notice rufous on the upperparts.  Legs were pink.       Aged as an immature based on the rather streaky crown and some sparse streaking on the sides.
